mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation expectations, which erodes the real value of fixed-income securities and compels investors to demand higher nominal yields, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year rank correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and subsequent 20-day yield direction is less than 0.10; a two-sided t-test on the mean yield change in months with rising CPI shows no significant difference from zero at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 5.5%, the economy operates with significant labor slack, suppressing wage growth and inflation expectations; this depresses nominal yields, leading to lower 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the mean 20-day yield return during triggered periods (UNRATE>5.5) is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A scenario where industrial production (INDPRO) is rising while the CPI (CPIAUCSL) is falling indicates that output growth is supply-driven and non-inflationary; this reduces inflation expectations and, consequently, 10-year yields decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL falls simultaneously, the median 20-day yield return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over a 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery phase (high unemployment, low policy rates), an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals consumer confidence and residential investment, which drives economic growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE above 5.5% and FEDFUNDS below 1%, the rank IC between month-over-month HOUST change and subsequent 20-day yield direction is below 0.10 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, a primary growth driver; this strengthens real growth expectations and lifts the 10-year yield over a short 5-day horizon due to fast market adjustment.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward yield return after months with positive RSAFS growth is not significantly greater than after months with negative RSAFS growth (two-sample t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.25% from the near-zero level, it signals the beginning of a monetary tightening cycle; expectations of future rate hikes increase long-term yields, lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following any FEDFUNDS rate hike that pushes it above 0.25%, the mean 60-day forward yield return is not positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or the positive hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a robust labor market that boosts aggregate demand and inflation expectations; this combination drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, the median 20-day yield return is not positive or the proportion of positive returns is below 55% over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime (FEDFUNDS near 0%), a rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) is interpreted by markets as an escape from deflation, leading to expectations of eventual policy normalization; this pushes long-term yields upward over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the rank IC between month-over-month CPIAUCSL change and subsequent 20-day yield direction is below 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.
